title: Honeywell CEO Kapur Says Aligned With Elliott From the Start

title: Stocks Climb as Chip Rout Stalls and Oil Slips: Markets Wrap
summary: A rout that sent chip stocks into a bear market paused at the start of a week in which traders will look to Big Tech earnings to gauge whether volatility persists or momentum returns. Brent erased early gains.

title: Philippine Senate Seeks VP Duterte’s Bank, Tax Records in Trial
summary: The Philippine Senate impeachment court will subpoena the bank and tax records of Vice President Sara Duterte as her opponents seek to oust the nation’s second-highest official on allegations that include corruption and unexplained wealth.

title: SBI Funds Pays Razor-Thin Banker Fees on Top India IPO of 2026
summary: The nine investment banks that manage SBI Funds Management Ltd.‘s initial public offering, India‘s first billion-dollar debut this year, will receive one of the worst payouts on a large debut in the country.

title: Citi Says Magnificent Seven Tag Is Obsolete in AI Stock Winners
summary: The popular Magnificent Seven stocks moniker is “no longer relevant” in assessing how to play the US artificial intelligence trade, according to Citigroup Inc. strategists.

title: South Korea Details Plan to Make Won Freely Tradable Abroad
summary: South Korea laid out a detailed plan to make the won freely tradable among foreigners, its boldest step yet to liberalize the foreign-exchange market.

title: Latest Oil Market News and Analysis for July 20
summary: Oil erased an earlier increase after Iran’s Foreign Ministry said it had received proposals from mediators regarding the war with the US.

title: Gold Steadies as Traders Assess Rates Path Amid US-Iran Conflict
summary: Gold steadied after escalating US-Iran clashes over the weekend were followed by reports of mediation efforts, with traders assessing the likelihood of the Federal Reserve raising interest rates to contain inflation.

title: Segro Rejects £13.5 Billion Cash and Share Prologis Offer
summary: Prologis Inc. has increased its outline bid to buy Segro Plc to £13.5 billion ($18.2 billion) and offered a series of sweeteners as it seeks to tempt shareholders after the UK landlord’s board rejected the latest terms.

title: Bond Traders Take Warsh at Word, See Inflation Fight Continuing
summary: Bond traders and Federal Reserve Chairman Kevin Warsh are in agreement on a crucial point: The central bank’s fight against inflation still seems far from over.

title: US Gasoline Prices Surpass $4 a Gallon for First Time in a Month
summary: US gasoline prices climbed back over the $4-a-gallon mark at the pump as the conflict in the Middle East escalated, threatening to exacerbate a global crunch for transportation fuels and stoke inflation.

title: Tungsten Miner’s Stock Jumps as Billionaire Forrest Buys Stake
summary: EQ Resources Ltd. shares surged after mining billionaire Andrew Forrest agreed to acquire a 16.8% stake in the Sydney-listed tungsten producer from Oaktree Capital Management LP.

title: Rising Demand for Hedges Hints at Volatility Ahead: Taking Stock
summary: The momentum trade carried along by the equity rally is faltering, while optimistic earnings expectations are under intense scrutiny, two of the signs pointing to a volatile summer.

title: Palm Climbs to Near One-Month High as Iran Conflict Boosts Crude
summary: Palm oil rose to the highest in nearly a month, tracking renewed strength in crude and other vegetable oil prices as the US and Iran exchanged escalating military strikes.

title: China Is Exporting 20% Fewer Magnets to US Despite Trade Truce
summary: China’s shipments of rare-earth magnets to the US remain well below pre-trade war levels despite last year’s truce, underscoring concerns in the Trump administration about Beijing’s adherence to that agreement.

title: Octopus-Backed MOPO Partners Nigeria in $75 Million Battery Plan
summary: MOPO, an Africa-focused battery rental company backed by Octopus Energy Group Ltd., said it’s signed an agreement with Nigeria to facilitate a $75 million expansion of its activities in Africa’s most populous nation.
